failures.test: optimistic-ui.spec.ts:9 — page navigation renders with transition
failures.error: expect(locator).toBeVisible failed: locator('h1:has-text("Alerts")') - element(s) not found
failures.hypothesis: The Alerts page uses <Breadcrumbs /> component with no <h1> element. Test expects h1:has-text("Alerts") which doesn't exist in DOM.
failures.suggested_fix: Replace `page.locator('h1:has-text("Alerts")')` with breadcrumb-based or page.waitForURL(/\/alerts/) check. The page renders successfully but has no h1.
failures.analysis: Verified: apps/web/src/app/alerts/page.tsx uses Breadcrumbs component (renders <nav> with <span> elements) and h2 section headers. No h1 exists. Test selector is incorrect.

failures.test: responsive.spec.ts:45 — mobile nav first tap should navigate immediately
failures.error: strict mode violation: getByRole('link', { name: /^settings$/i }) resolved to 2 elements
failures.hypothesis: Locator getByRole('link', { name: /^settings$/i }) matches both sidebar nav link AND a breadcrumb/topbar link. Need more specific selector.
failures.suggested_fix: Use data-testid='nav-item-settings' or page.locator('nav[aria-label="Mobile navigation"] a[href="/settings"]') to target only the sidebar link.
failures.analysis: Screenshot shows both sidebar nav-item-settings and a topbar Settings link exist. Strict mode correctly rejects ambiguous selector.

notes: Both failures are test-bugs (incorrect selectors), NOT code regressions. Fix1 changes (sidebar items, tRPC httpLink) are working correctly. All auth, navigation, CRUD, RBAC, responsive layout, theme, and core feature tests pass.
